Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Defendant Union Carbide Corporation¿S Special Interrogatories
Off Calendar Oct-27-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEARANCES: Paul Bird, Atty. for defendant and moving party: Union Carbide Corporation; Rett D. Bergmark, Atty. for Plaintiff and Opposing party: Keith Simmons

RULING: HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED: Defendant Union Carbide Corporation's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Special Interrogatories is granted. Plaintiffs' objections are overruled and ordered stricken. Reference to other responses is inadequate. Each response must be made separately and must be full and complete at the time it is made. Plaintiff is ordered to respond further within 15 days of notice of this order.

Plaintiffs responses were a misuse of the discovery process. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied as no facts were set forth in a declaration which supports an amount of any monetary sanctions being sought. [See C.C.P. Section 2023.040.]

Plaintiffs' opposition to the motion as being improperly calendared to be heard by a Court Commissioner has no merit. Plaintiff's objection to a commissioner hearing discovery was not timely filed as it was filed on May 14, 2009, more than 30 days after their appearance on the complaint which was filed January 13, 2009. [See Local Rule of Court 10 D which further directs that despite any objection, oral argument on the motion shall be heard in the Discovery Department.]Order was signed in open court. COMMISSIONER EVERETT A. HEWLETT, JR. REPORTER: Rita R. Lerner, CSR# 3179
